title: What Oceanus Remembers
tagline: The Titan mentioned Oceanus once, briefly, in EP021. Oceanus has been in the Olympus Sea the whole time. He has been listening to everything.
hook_line: Oceanus first appeared in EP021 — a brief surface, a wave, gone. The team noted him and moved on. He is still there. He has been in the Olympus Sea for as long as anyone can remember. He is older than the Titan. He watched the Titan go underground. He was there when it came back. He has been listening to the dolphin messages the whole time. Today, at low tide, he surfaces and talks to Poseidon directly.

scenes.title: Oceanus Surfaces
scenes.narration: Poseidon was at his usual morning monitoring position — the eastern dock, log open, good quill, sardine on the note. The tide was low. In low tide, the Olympus Sea was quieter, the surface calmer. At low tide, things that were usually underwater were briefly visible. Oceanus surfaced at low tide. Not in the way the Titan surfaced — the Titan was large, the Titan's surfacing was an event. Oceanus surfaced the way very old things surface: slowly, with great deliberateness, taking the time required. He was older than the Titan. His features were the sea itself — not carved into it, made of it. He looked at Poseidon. He said: 'You have been busy.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon at the eastern dock, morning monitoring position — low tide, the sea surface calm and clear — Oceanus surfacing slowly from the Olympus Sea: enormous but different from the Titan, his form made of water-patterns rather than carved stone — ancient, much older than the Titan, older than the arrangement of the sea around him — surfacing deliberately, without hurry — looking at Poseidon directly — 'you have been busy.' — Poseidon looking up from his log, quill in hand, sardine on the note — the specific quality of meeting someone for a real conversation who has been present the entire time — low tide light, the sea very clear, #4DB6E8 and deep ocean blue, Oceanus's water-form as the dominant visual

scenes.title: What Oceanus Has Heard
scenes.narration: Poseidon said: 'You've been listening to the dolphin messages.' 'Yes,' Oceanus said. 'The dolphins speak in my water. I hear what they carry.' 'For how long?' Oceanus considered this. 'I have been in this sea since before the sea had a name. I heard the Titan go underground. I heard it settle. I heard you find the river.' He paused. 'That was a good day.' Poseidon looked at his log. He had notes on all of it. He had not known Oceanus was listening. 'What do you want to say?' Poseidon asked. 'I want to say,' Oceanus said, 'that the Titan has been underground a very long time. Longer than it told you.' He paused. 'It told you a number. The number is accurate. But the weight of that number — I don't think you have felt it.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Oceanus and Poseidon's first real exchange — 'the dolphins speak in my water. I hear what they carry.' — the revelation: Oceanus has been listening since before the sea had a name — 'I heard the Titan go underground. I heard it settle. I heard you find the river. That was a good day.' — Poseidon's log open, notes on everything Oceanus is describing — 'what do you want to say?' — Oceanus's statement: 'the Titan has been underground a very long time. Longer than it told you. The number is accurate. But the weight of that number — I don't think you have felt it.' — Poseidon looking at the Titan's shape in the sea — the weight of 'longer than it told you': not a correction, a context — #4DB6E8 and the low tide clarity, Oceanus and the Titan's silhouette both visible

scenes.title: The Weight of the Number
scenes.narration: Poseidon asked Athena to come to the dock. She came with her scrolls. Oceanus regarded her the way very old things regard very intelligent young things: with interest, and patience. Athena said: 'The Titan said it went underground a long time ago.' 'Yes,' Oceanus said. 'How long is long?' Oceanus said: 'When the Titan went underground, the coastline of the Olympus Sea was different. The eastern valley did not look east — it looked south. The river that is now underground was the main river of a larger river system that has since changed course entirely.' Athena was writing. 'How different?' 'Different enough,' Oceanus said, 'that if you showed the Titan a map of today's sea, it would recognize the water but not the shore.' He paused. 'I think that is part of why it has been careful.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena arriving at the dock with her scrolls — Oceanus regarding her with ancient-wise patience — Athena's direct question: 'how long is long?' — Oceanus's answer: the coastline was different, the eastern valley faced south, the underground river was a main river of a larger system — Athena writing all of this, her hand moving fast — 'if you showed the Titan a map of today's sea, it would recognize the water but not the shore' — the weight of this: the Titan came back to a world that has changed more than it knew — 'I think that is part of why it has been careful' — the care the Titan has been showing — trying not to be too heavy — understood in a new context — Poseidon's expression shifting — the scroll filling with Athena's notes — #4DB6E8 and the dock, Oceanus in the low-tide clarity

scenes.title: Oceanus and the Titan
scenes.narration: Poseidon asked: 'Do you talk to the Titan?' 'I have,' Oceanus said. 'Not through dolphins. Directly.' 'Since it came back?' 'Since the first night. It was frightened,' Oceanus said. 'Not of anything specific. Frightened in the way you are frightened when you wake up somewhere that was the right place when you went to sleep and is different now.' He paused. 'I told it the water was the same. The water has not changed. The water is always the same water.' Poseidon looked at the sea. 'Did that help?' 'Yes,' Oceanus said. 'And then the dolphins started bringing messages. And that helped more.' He looked at Poseidon. 'What you have been doing — the correspondence, the packages, the sculpture — that has been the right thing.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon's question about the Titan — Oceanus confirming he talks to the Titan directly, not through dolphins — 'the first night. It was frightened. Not of anything specific. Frightened in the way you are when you wake somewhere that was right when you went to sleep and is different now.' — Oceanus's reassurance to the Titan: 'the water is the same. The water has not changed.' — and then the dolphin correspondence beginning to help more — Oceanus looking at Poseidon: 'what you have been doing — the correspondence, the packages, the sculpture — that has been the right thing.' — Poseidon receiving this acknowledgment from the oldest thing in the sea — his expression: moved — the sardine still on his log note — #4DB6E8 and the sea and the morning light, the Titan visible in the background

scenes.title: What Oceanus Wants to Give
scenes.narration: Oceanus said: 'I have something I would like to send the Titan through your correspondence. If you would carry it.' 'Yes,' Poseidon said immediately. 'What is it?' 'A map,' Oceanus said. 'Of the coastline as it was when the Titan went underground. And the coastline now. So it can see both.' He paused. 'I have been carrying both coastlines for long enough that the comparison is — manageable. For me. I do not know how it will feel for the Titan.' 'It might be difficult,' Athena said. 'Yes,' Oceanus said. 'I think so. But I think it should know. It is better to see the change than to be surprised by it slowly.' He looked at Athena. 'You agree.' It was not a question. 'Yes,' Athena said.
scenes.visual_prompt: Oceanus's offer: a map of both coastlines — as it was, as it is — for the Titan — 'I have been carrying both coastlines for long enough that the comparison is manageable for me. I do not know how it will feel for the Titan.' — Athena: 'it might be difficult' / Oceanus: 'yes. But it should know. Better to see the change than to be surprised by it slowly.' — 'you agree' — not a question — Athena: 'yes' — the two of them in alignment on this: the harder true thing over the comfortable incomplete thing — Poseidon between them, accepting the responsibility of carrying the map — the sea around Oceanus beginning to form the shape of something — the two coastlines, past and present, emerging briefly in the water around his form — #4DB6E8 and the water-map visual, past and present coastlines

scenes.title: The Map Goes to the Titan
scenes.narration: Oceanus gave Poseidon the map — not on paper, drawn in shells arranged on the dock by the tide, then transferred to Athena's careful notation. The dolphin relay carried it to the Titan. The team waited. The Titan's reply came back in segments, like the longer messages did. First segment: a long silence — twenty minutes before the dolphin swam back. Then: 'I SEE. YES. THE VALLEY.' A pause, another dolphin. 'I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT THE VALLEY.' Another pause. 'IT WAS SOUTH BEFORE.' And then, after the longest gap, the final message: 'THE WATER IS THE SAME.' Poseidon looked at Oceanus in the sea. Oceanus had not left during the wait. 'Yes,' Oceanus said. 'It is the same.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Oceanus giving the map via shell arrangement on the dock — Athena notating it carefully — the dolphin relay carrying it — the team waiting: Poseidon, Athena, Zeus (who arrived during the wait) — the Titan's segmented reply across twenty minutes: silence, then 'I SEE. YES. THE VALLEY.' / 'I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT THE VALLEY.' / 'IT WAS SOUTH BEFORE.' / and then the long gap — and finally: 'THE WATER IS THE SAME.' — Poseidon looking at Oceanus, who has stayed in the water the whole wait — Oceanus: 'yes. It is the same.' — the Titan arriving at the same comfort Oceanus offered on the first night: the water doesn't change — the shore changed, the valley changed, but the water — Poseidon's sardine note with this entire exchange written in it — #4DB6E8 and the dock and the sea, the dolphin relay visible

scenes.title: Archie Was at the Dock
scenes.narration: Archie had flown to the dock during the wait. He was on the dock post when the final message came in. He had watched Oceanus surface, watched the conversation, watched the dolphin relays, watched Poseidon's face through all of it. When the final message came — THE WATER IS THE SAME — Archie looked at Oceanus. Oceanus looked at Archie. This lasted three seconds. Then Archie flew back toward Olympus. Oceanus descended slowly, back into the sea, with the same deliberateness with which he had surfaced. Poseidon wrote in his log: 'The water is the same. This is still true.' He added a sardine. He looked at the sea for a while before walking back.
scenes.visual_prompt: Archie having arrived at the dock during the wait — on the dock post, watching everything — the final message arriving: 'THE WATER IS THE SAME' — Archie looking at Oceanus — Oceanus looking at Archie — three seconds — Archie flying back toward Olympus — Oceanus descending slowly into the sea, the same deliberateness as his surfacing — Poseidon writing in his log: 'The water is the same. This is still true.' — the sardine added — Poseidon standing at the dock looking at the sea, the Titan's shape visible, Oceanus gone below the surface — the morning having become afternoon during this — the dock as the space where this all happened — #4DB6E8 and the sea light, Poseidon's lone figure on the dock with his log

viral_hook: Oceanus has been in the Olympus Sea since before it had a name. He's been listening to every dolphin message. He told Poseidon: the Titan came back to a world where the coastline had changed, the valley used to face south, the underground river was the main river of a system that no longer exists. The Titan's reply when it saw the comparison map: 'THE WATER IS THE SAME.' Oceanus said: 'Yes. It is.'
highlight_note: The Titan's segmented reply after seeing the map of past and present coastlines — silence, then: 'I SEE. YES. THE VALLEY.' / 'I DID NOT KNOW ABOUT THE VALLEY.' / 'IT WAS SOUTH BEFORE.' / long gap / 'THE WATER IS THE SAME.' — Oceanus waiting in the sea the whole time: 'yes. It is.'
short_title: The Titan Saw Both Coastlines and the Water Is the Same
rewatch_bait: Oceanus in EP021 — a brief surface, a wave, gone. He's been in the sea the whole season listening. He heard the dolphin messages. He heard Poseidon find the river. He said 'that was a good day.'
youtube_title: Oceanus surfaced and told Poseidon: the Titan came back to a shore it doesn't recognize. Then it found the thing that hasn't changed. | Little Olympus S2 EP15
lesson: When everything has changed, sometimes the best thing to show someone is what hasn't. The coastline is different. The valley changed direction. But the water is the same water. That was enough.
arc_notes: Oceanus formally introduced — has been in the sea since before it was named, listened to every dolphin message, spoke to the Titan on its first night. The Titan's fear on return: waking somewhere that was right when you went to sleep and is different now. Oceanus's consolation: the water is the same. Athena's map exchange: the first time the Titan understands how much has changed while it was underground. 'THE WATER IS THE SAME' is the Titan's most significant emotional statement. Archie-Oceanus three-second look: the two observers, the two watchers. Poseidon's log note: 'The water is the same. This is still true.'
